skill.name: Azure AI Search SDK for Python
skill.description: Azure AI Search SDK for Python. Use for vector search, hybrid search, semantic ranking, indexing, and skillsets.
skill.summary: Full-text, vector, and hybrid search with AI enrichment capabilities for Azure.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill providing guidance for Azure AI Search Python SDK usage. Static analysis detected no executable code (0 files scanned, risk score 0/100). Code examples follow security best practices including environment variable usage for credentials and recommendation of DefaultAzureCredential over API keys. No malicious patterns or prompt injection attempts detected.

content.user_title: Implement Azure AI Search with Python SDK
content.value_statement: Build powerful search experiences with Azure AI Search. This skill provides comprehensive guidance on vector search, hybrid search, semantic ranking, and index management using the official Python SDK.
actual_capabilities: Guide index creation with vector field configuration and HNSW algorithm settings
Demonstrate hybrid search combining vector and keyword queries
Show semantic ranking with captions and answer extraction
Explain authentication patterns including API key and DefaultAzureCredential
Cover indexer and skillset creation for AI enrichment pipelines
Provide async patterns and error handling strategies
limitations: Requires an Azure AI Search service instance to be provisioned
Python-specific guidance, does not cover other language SDKs
Assumes Azure environment or configured credentials for authentication
Not a replacement for official Azure documentation

use_cases.title: Build Vector Search for RAG Applications
use_cases.description: Create vector indexes and perform semantic similarity search for retrieval-augmented generation pipelines using Azure OpenAI embeddings.

use_cases.title: Implement Enterprise Document Search
use_cases.description: Set up hybrid search with keyword and vector queries, semantic ranking, and faceted navigation for document repositories.

use_cases.title: Create AI Enrichment Pipelines
use_cases.description: Configure indexers with skillsets to extract entities, key phrases, and perform OCR on documents during indexing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Search Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to perform a basic keyword search using Azure AI Search Python SDK with proper authentication.

prompt_templates.title: Vector Index Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a search index with vector fields configured for 1536-dimensional embeddings using HNSW algorithm.

prompt_templates.title: Hybrid Search Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write code to perform hybrid search combining keyword matching with vector similarity, returning top 10 results with semantic captions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced search with multiple ranking signals

prompt_templates.title: Indexer with Skillset
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an indexer that reads from Azure Blob Storage and applies entity recognition and key phrase extraction skillsets before indexing.

output_examples.input: Search for documents about semantic search with vector similarity
output_examples.output: Returns ranked results with relevance scores, semantic captions highlighting matching content, and document metadata

output_examples.input: Create index with vector field for 3072-dimensional embeddings
output_examples.output: Index configuration with SearchField for embeddings, HNSW algorithm settings, and vector search profile
Successfully indexed documents with buffered sender handling retries and batch optimization

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production authentication instead of API keys
Enable semantic ranking configuration for natural language query understanding
Use SearchIndexingBufferedSender for batch uploads to handle automatic batching and retries
anti_patterns: Hardcoding credentials or endpoints directly in code instead of using environment variables
Using API key authentication in production environments instead of Entra ID
Creating indexes without vector search profiles when planning to use embeddings

faq.question: What authentication method should I use for Azure AI Search?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential (Entra ID) for production environments. API keys are acceptable for development but should not be used in production due to security concerns.

faq.question: How do I choose between SearchClient and SearchIndexClient?
faq.answer: Use SearchClient for querying and document operations. Use SearchIndexClient for creating, updating, or deleting index definitions and managing index-level configurations.

faq.question: What vector dimensions should I configure for my embeddings?
faq.answer: Match the dimensions to your embedding model output. Common sizes are 1536 (text-embedding-ada-002), 3072 (text-embedding-3-large), or 768 (smaller models).

faq.question: Can I add vector fields to an existing index?
faq.answer: No, vector search configuration must be defined at index creation time. Create a new index with vector fields if you need to add vector search to an existing solution.

faq.question: How does hybrid search improve results?
faq.answer: Hybrid search combines keyword matching (BM25) with vector similarity, leveraging both lexical and semantic relevance for more accurate results than either approach alone.

faq.question: What is the difference between semantic ranking and vector search?
faq.answer: Vector search finds similar documents using embeddings. Semantic ranking uses LLM-powered re-ranking to improve result ordering for natural language queries, working on top of keyword or hybrid search.
